title: Image Size, Build Performance, Runtime Efficiency, and Reproducibility · Image and Runtime Lens: Docker Runtime Resource Profile
summary: Build time and image size doubled after dependency changes, while production startup and memory regress despite a warm local cache. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker runtime resource profile increment in measured build and runtime optimization experiment. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Benchmark startup, steady CPU, memory, I/O, network, process count, and shutdown under representative limits and changed load.
Compare trusted base, distroless or scratch, packages, debugging needs, certificates, locales, dynamic libraries, and patch workflow.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.

version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment

steps.title: Predict Docker Runtime Resource Profile · Theory Docker Runtime Resource Profile
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team compares a warm local build with a cold CI build and blames a large final image, while context transfer and cross-platform cache dominate. Measure each stage and compare controlled output digests.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Docker Runtime Resource Profile contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name client, daemon, builder, image, container, host-kernel, storage, network, registry, and supply-chain boundaries; predict state and authority; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, cleanup, and live-environment transfer limit. Apply that move specifically to Docker Runtime Resource Profile while producing measured build and runtime optimization experiment.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example team compares a warm local build with a cold CI build and blames a large final image, while context transfer and cross-platform cache dominate. Measure each stage and compare controlled output digests. Competency probe: Benchmark startup, steady CPU, memory, I/O, network, process count, and shutdown under representative limits and changed load. A defensible solution applies Docker Runtime Resource Profile, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to measured build and runtime optimization experiment.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-runtime-resource-profile — Benchmark startup, steady CPU, memory, I/O, network, process count, and shutdown under representative limits and changed load.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-performance-reproducibility-3
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-performance-reproducibility-3
    artifact: build-performance-budget.yaml
    decision: worked docker performance reproducibility 3
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: IPv6-enabled Compose project on a shared CI runner
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      context-bytes: measured
      cache-hit-rate: observed-not-assumed
      cold-build: recorded
      image-bytes: per-platform
      runtime-budget: cpu-memory-io
      reproducibility: digest-compared
    verify: docker buildx du && docker image inspect candidate --format json
    failure: Reject image size as the sole performance metric, warm-cache timing as a cold build, or equal source commits as proof of equal image digests.
    repair: Measure context, transfer, layers, cache, startup, CPU, memory, and I/O separately; change one variable and compare digests under controlled inputs.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Container overhead is always negligible and needs no measurement. This fails the Docker Runtime Resource Profile contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
